items.title: Penang aims for 100pc cashless transactions
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 21 — The Penang government is committed to achieving 100 per cent cashless transactions in the near future through various initiatives currently being implemented. State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said one of the key initiatives involves the participation of all 24 state government departments and agencies in a campaign promoting digital payments to expand the adoption of cashless transactions among the public. He said the achievement marked Penang’s second consecutive win, following its 95 per cent cashless transaction rate under the previous Cashless Boleh 4.0 campaign.

items.title: UN: Scam losses in Asian regions up to US$114bil in 2025
items.summary: BANGKOK: Victims of transnational online scams in East Asia, Southeast Asia, Australia and New Zealand lost up to US$114.1 billion last year, a UN report said on Tuesday, with estimated losses at least tripling from 2023. Online scams, mostly from investment fraud, across the specified regions cost victims an estimated US$88.3 billion to US$114.1 billion last year, according to the new report from the UN Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC). The combined losses far exceed the US$18 billion-US$37 billion estimated to have been lost in 2023, and reflect "the dramatic scaling of this criminal economy", it said.

items.title: Police bust international scam syndicate targeting US citizens in Kuantan raid
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 21 —Police arrested 16 individuals, including eight women, believed to be involved in an online scam syndicate during a raid on a premises near Jalan Tengku Muhammad here yesterday. Acting Pahang police chief DCP Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the suspects, comprising 12 Philippine nationals, two locals, and two Taiwanese nationals aged between 24 and 51, were nabbed at about 11 pm. “Preliminary investigations found that the two Taiwanese men were the masterminds, while the two local men acted as runners and drivers.

items.title: Central Asia's unbridled cosmetic surgery boom
items.summary: AFTER two pregnancies, Diana Tergenbayeva dreamed of a breast lift. Instead, she was left with severe swelling, an open wound and a legal dispute with the surgeon who performed her botched procedure in Kyrgyzstan. Tergenbayeva's surgeon was detained in September for smuggling medications into the country.

items.title: Smugglers hide RM577,280 worth of cigarettes in remote village hut
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Smugglers are turning remote village huts into hidden storage facilities for contraband cigarettes before distributing them to local buyers. Kelantan Customs director Mohd Hisham Mohd Nor said the tactic was uncovered after the department's Rantau Panjang enforcement team raided a hut in Kampung Kubang Bulat, Gunong, Bachok, on June 29. Hisham said initial investigations found that the hut was used to temporarily store the cigarettes before they were distributed to local buyers.

items.title: India youth group says will not march again because police will 'hurt youth'
items.summary: NEW DELHI: India's youth-led "cockroach" movement said on Tuesday it would continue its protest but will not march again, a day after scores of supporters were injured in clashes with police who tried to stop their march on parliament. Delhi Police said nearly 180 people, including 118 security and police personnel, and 60 protesters, were injured on Monday when they clashed in central Delhi. Early on Tuesday, about 150-200 protesters gathered at the protest site of Jantar Mantar in central Delhi, chanting slogans amid a mild drizzle.

items.title: Tabung Haji in much stronger financial position following recovery efforts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) is in a much stronger financial position now compared with 2018 following continuous recovery and restructuring efforts. TH recorded a significant increase in profit distribution rate to 3.5 per cent for the 2025 financial year (FY2025) compared with 1.25 per cent for FY2018, benefiting more than 9 million depositors. According to TH, the recovery and restructuring measures undertaken in 2018 were strategic decisions aimed at safeguarding depositors' savings and ensuring the fund's long-term financial sustainability.

items.title: [UPDATED] Sabah-based NGO president arrested over alleged RM2 million misappropriation
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The president of a Sabah-based non-governmental organisation (NGO) has been arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) for allegedly misappropriating RM2 million allocated for the construction of a cultural hall. The male suspect, in his 60s, was arrested at about 10am today after turning up to give his statement at the Sabah MACC office. A source said preliminary investigations revealed that the suspect allegedly committed the offence in 2022 by misappropriating funds obtained from the Finance Ministry for the construction of a cultural hall and gallery in the state.

items.title: GOF detains driver after car chase, seizes air rifle and ball bearings
items.summary: TUMPAT: A 32-year-old man was arrested after he allegedly rammed a General Operations Force (GOF) vehicle while attempting to evade arrest in Kampung Parit Bendang Morak here yesterday. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the suspect was detained by a GOF team at 5.30am. Radzi said the suspect also claimed the car belonged to his father.

items.title: Smart manufacturing fund to unlock SME growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry and the Securities Commission (SC) have launched a new Smart Manufacturing scheme to provide greater access to capital and support small and medium enterprises (SMEs) and mid-tier companies (MTCs) in strategic sectors. The scheme, under the New Industrial Master Plan 2030's (NIMP 2030) Strategic Co-Investment Fund (NIMP CoSIF), is aimed at supporting business expansion and accelerating smart manufacturing adoption in strategic industries through enhanced government co-investment. The move also aligns the funding ratio for "Other Sectors" with that of the priority sectors and new growth sectors under NIMP 2030.

items.title: [UPDATED] NGO secretary, treasurer arrested over alleged RM5 million money laundering
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A non-governmental organisation (NGO) secretary and treasurer have been arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) for allegedly laundering RM5 million in funds. The source said the alleged money laundering involved funds amounting to about RM5 million. MACC Anti-Money Laundering Division director Datuk Norhaizam Muhammad confirmed the arrests when contacted.

items.title: French lawmakers vote on social media ban for children
items.summary: With the proposed legislation, children younger than 15 would not be allowed to open a social media account from Sept 1. Social media platforms would have another four months to close accounts already open. Social media platforms generally oppose blanket bans and stress they already have measures to protect younger users, including age restrictions.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends lower on profit-taking despite positive market breadth
items.summary: Among active stocks, Main Market debutant Stratus Global surged RM1.31 to RM2.11, VS Industry added 2.5 sen to 25 sen, Zetrix AI edged up one sen to 73 sen, Dagang NeXchange gained two sen to 46.5 sen, and Aimax was unchanged at one sen. Among top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries garnered RM2.18 to RM46.84, Mi Technovation soared RM1.05 sen to RM5.92, Nestle advanced 46 sen to RM93.12, UMS Integration rose by 41 sen to RM8.19, and Unisem was 37 sen higher at RM4.85. United Plantations led the decliners, slipping 86 sen to RM33.74, Kuala Lumpur Kepong dipped 44 sen to RM21.14, Fraser & Neave lost 34 sen to RM28.80, Petronas Gas fell 22 sen to RM17.40, and Petronas Dagangan erased 16 sen to RM19.50.

items.title: Britain's new PM to cut taxes on electricity bills
items.summary: BENGALURU: The government of new British Prime Minister Andy Burnham will cut taxes on electricity bills, Downing Street said in a statement on Tuesday, as he attempts to deliver on his promises to ease a cost-of-living crisis. The British government is planning to remove the value-added tax (VAT) from domestic electricity bills from Oct 1, the statement said. The cost of this action for this financial year will be funded from the cancellation of the government's £1.8 billion (US$2.42 billion) digital ID programme, Downing Street said.

items.title: MGB wins RM44.7mil Saudi contract, second direct award from ROSHN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MGB Bhd has won a RM44.67 million contract in Saudi Arabia for mechanical, electrical and plumbing (MEP) and finishing works at the Marafy Al Arous residential development in Jeddah, strengthening its presence in the kingdom's fast-growing construction sector. MGB, a construction, property development and industrialised building system (IBS) precast concrete manufacturer, said the repeat award reflects ROSHN's continued confidence in the group's project delivery capabilities while reinforcing its expansion strategy in Saudi Arabia. Tan Sri Dr Lim Hock San, the group executive chairman of MGB, said this latest award from ROSHN marks another positive milestone in MGB's progress within Saudi Arabia.

items.title: Education must produce critical thinkers, not just AI-ready graduates, says Zambry
items.summary: Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said this was why Malaysia's higher education system must remain firmly rooted in humanity and ethics, even as universities accelerate efforts to prepare graduates for an increasingly AI-driven future. The purpose of education is to cultivate the mind. "Education teaches people how to think critically about everything around them.

items.title: Zahid: PM can sack BN ministers if unhappy with performance
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim can dismiss ministers from the coalition if he is unhappy with their performance. Zahid, who is also the deputy prime minister, said there was no reason for BN ministers to resign voluntarily. "The prime minister warned that those who speak ill of other ministers or ministries during the election campaign should resign," Zahid said at the Amanat TPM event with the Negri Sembilan Community Development Department today.

items.title: Nearly 12,000 Cambodians seek medical care in Malaysia: MHTC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nearly 12,000 Cambodian patients sought medical treatment in Malaysia in 2025, contributing RM30 million in healthcare travel revenue. The Malaysia Healthcare Travel Council (MHTC) said Cambodia's growing trust in the country's healthcare reflects rising demand for quality overseas medical treatment. MHTC said Cambodia's healthcare landscape has evolved significantly over the past decade, with rising healthcare awareness and growing demand for specialised treatments driving more patients to seek cross-border healthcare services.

items.title: Documents in fatal MEX crash handed over to army captain's lawyer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The prosecution today handed over documents related to two charges against an army captain linked to a drink-driving crash that killed a Bangladeshi man on Maju Expressway (MEX) in April. Lawyer Muhammad Nazreen Jaafar Abdullah, who is representing Jad Faid Arhan, 31, confirmed receiving the chemist report, his client's alcohol level results, medical reports of the crash victims and photographs of the accident scene. After the proceedings, Nazreen said he was still awaiting several documents from the prosecution at another magistrate's court in relation to a murder charge involving the death of Bangladeshi Muzahid Millad, 22, a popular gaming content creator known as "Advance Gaming".

items.title: Ahmad Maslan: BN to battle PH in states, partners at federal level
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional will continue to compete against Pakatan Harapan at the state level while maintaining its partnership with the coalition at the federal level. Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said this dynamic reflects a new political maturity, even if it appears somewhat unusual. Although we are not aligned at the state level, we remain together in the federal government," he told reporters after visiting the Communications Ministry Media Centre here today.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Rival parties share neighbourly spirit despite Juasseh three-cornered contest
items.summary: A visit to Juasseh Tengah found that the operations centres of Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Bersatu are separated by only about eight steps. Directly opposite them is Barisan Nasional's (BN) operations centre, about 20 steps from both PH and Bersatu. Trader Norizan Mohamed Yaacob, 51, said the close proximity of the three operations centres reflected the cordial relationship among party workers despite the keen electoral contest.

items.title: KTMB adds six KTM Komuter Northern Sector services from Friday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) will introduce an improved KTM Komuter Northern Sector timetable from Friday, adding six daily services to cater to growing passenger demand and enhance travel comfort, particularly during peak hours. KTMB said waiting times on the Ipoh-Butterworth route during the morning peak would be reduced to 55 minutes from more than 120 minutes previously. KTMB said waiting times on the Padang Besar-Butterworth route during the evening peak would be halved to 30 minutes from 60 minutes.

items.title: Zico Trust partners Suriana to support disadvantaged children in Desa Mentari
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Zico Trust (M) Berhad has adopted Pusat Akademik dan Latihan 5 (PAL 5), a community learning centre in Desa Mentari, to provide holistic educational and psychosocial support for disadvantaged children. The initiative, managed by Suriana Welfare Society Malaysia, will see Zico Trust funding a comprehensive suite of programmes including academic enrichment, digital learning, life skills development, and community engagement. Zico Trust (M) Berhad chief financial officer Dinesh Menon said the firm was honoured to play a role in creating tangible opportunities for the B40 community.

items.title: Aminuddin urges voters to study Pakatan Harapan's state election manifesto
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Pakatan Harapan hopes voters in Negri Sembilan will closely examine its state election manifesto, which prioritises all segments of society, particularly the M40 and B40 income groups. "We certainly hope voters will look at and study the PH manifesto," he said during house-to-house campaigning at Ladang Sekang Linggi here today. Under its manifesto themed 'Kekal Harapan', PH outlined 10 key commitments, including expanding educational opportunities to the M40 group.

items.title: Sarawak boat tragedy: Three-year-old found dead, three missing after vessel capsizes in Baram
items.summary: MIRI, July 21 — A three-year-old child was found dead, while three others remain missing after a boat carrying the victims reportedly capsized in Sungai Seperot near Long San, Baram. The victim’s body was taken to the Long San Health Clinic,” the department said in a statement. According to the police report, the four victims, all from Kampung Long Selatong, had left by boat for the Long San Health Clinic yesterday (July 20) but failed to return home and could not be contacted.

items.title: 10 workers die in hydroelectric project tunnel collapse in northeast India
items.summary: HYDERABAD: At least 10 workers were killed after an under-construction tunnel at a hydroelectric project collapsed in India's northeastern state of Sikkim, with naturally occurring toxic gas hampering rescue operations, local media reported on Tuesday. About 25 workers were inside the tunnel when it collapsed on Monday at the National Hydroelectric Power Corporation's Teesta Stage VI Hydroelectric Project in Samardung village, Namchi district. Sikkim Chief Minister Prem Singh Tamang said in social media posts that he had informed Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah about the situation following the disaster.

items.title: Post-mortem report still pending in case of army captain charged over fatal MEX Highway crash involving Bangladeshi passenger
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The prosecution has completed handing over documents for the case involving a military captain accused of driving under the influence of alcohol and causing a fatal crash that killed a Bangladeshi e-hailing passenger. However, defence lawyer Muhammad Nazreen Jaafar Abdullah, who represents the accused, Captain Jad Faid Arhan, 31, said documents related to the alleged murder charge, including the victim’s chemical and medical reports, had yet to be received. On May 1, Jad Faid was charged with two counts of driving a white Ford Fiesta under the influence of alcohol, causing him to lose control of the vehicle.

items.title: 'Sit down!' Australian politician berates dog on live TV
items.summary: SYDNEY: A top lawmaker from Australia's far-right One Nation party has grabbed headlines after exploding at a misbehaving dog as he was grilled on live TV. "Sorry, it's the dog," he told the journalist. "Don't worry about the dog, just answer the question," replied journalist Sarah Ferguson, who was grilling Joyce on his party leader Pauline Hanson's recent meeting with far-right British figurehead Tommy Robinson.

items.title: Penang leads seabass aquaculture output, stresses MyGAP compliance
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: Penang produced 77 per cent of Malaysia's aquaculture seabass output in 2025, with authorities urging strict compliance with Good Aquaculture Practices (MyGAP). Adnan said Penang produced 44,596.46 metric tonnes of aquaculture seabass worth RM562.51 million in 2025, accounting for 77 per cent of the national output. The Department of Fisheries (DOF) recorded Malaysia's total seabass production at 58,221.38 metric tonnes worth RM755.27 million last year.

items.title: US-targeted scam syndicate busted in Kuantan: 14 foreigners among 16 detained
items.summary: The suspects, aged between 24 and 51, comprised eight women and eight men. Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said intelligence gathering and surveillance led police to raid the premises, where they arrested two Taiwanese men, 12 Filipinos and two Malaysian men. "The premises served as a call centre, where the suspects impersonated police officers to target unsuspecting victims in the United States.

items.title: Zahid urges BN to boost campaign momentum in Negri Sembilan polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called on the coalition to step up its campaign momentum, cautioning that BN enters the state election as the underdog. We are the underdogs, and so are our candidates," Zahid said after attending the 'Amanat TPM' event with the Negri Sembilan Community Development Department today. On allegations that BN was using government assets for campaigning, Zahid said: "Others have used them too.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in several states including Kedah, Selangor, Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit several areas across the country, including parts of Perlis, Kedah, Perak, Selangor, Johor and Sarawak, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In an advisory issued at 3.45pm today, MetMalaysia said the weather conditions were expected to persist until 6pm in affected areas. In Selangor, affected areas include Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat, while Kuala Lumpur has also been placed under the thunderstorm warning.

items.title: Zahid tells BN machinery to work harder, admits party is ‘underdog’ in N. Sembilan polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has admitted he is not satisfied with the party’s preparations ahead of the 16th Negri Sembilan state election, urging its machinery and candidates to step up their efforts. Ahmad Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said BN’s candidates and election machinery must intensify their efforts throughout the campaign period to improve the party’s chances. At today’s event, Ahmad Zahid also presented Excellent Service Awards to recipients from the Kemas headquarters, Federal Territory, Negri Sembilan, Melaka, Kemas Kuala Lumpur Skills Institute (ILKKL) and Kemas Training Centre (PLK) Melaka.

items.title: Afghans search for dozens missing after devastating flood
items.summary: PARUN: Rescuers were struggling to reach residents trapped under mud and boulders in eastern Afghanistan on Tuesday, after a major flood smashed through one province killing at least 23 people. Around 100 people are unaccounted for, the national disaster agency and the provincial governor's office said, also updating the death toll from Monday's deluge. Farid Gul Zaheer, a 34-year-old pharmacist, said he had "never witnessed such a dangerous flood."

items.title: Kedah Sultan, Tunku Mahkota visit Media Prima
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sultan of Kedah, Al-Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah and the Tunku Mahkota of Kedah, Tunku Shazuddin Ariff Sultan Sallehuddin, tried their hand at reading the news during a visit to Balai Berita, Media Prima Berhad's (MPB) headquarters, today. The visit formed part of the Sultan and the Tunku Mahkota's tour of Balai Berita, which houses Media Prima's various media platforms, including television, print, digital and audio operations. [[nid:1494107]] Sultan Sallehuddin arrived shortly afterwards and was welcomed by Media Prima group chairman Datuk Seri Dr Syed Hussian Aljunid.

items.title: [UPDATED] Johor govt shuts down Network School, effective July 22
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor government has backed the Iskandar Puteri City Council's (MBIP) decision to revoke the business licence of Network School (NS0 Malaysia Sdn Bhd) and shut down all of its operations in Forest City, effective tomorrow. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the government fully supported MBIP's decision following a meeting to evaluate inspection reports, enforcement findings and written representations submitted by the company. On July 18, Regent of Johor Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim said the Johor government and local authorities would conduct a thorough investigation into issues surrounding Network School's operations in Forest City.

items.title: Forest City Network School licence revoked, Johor warns no one is above the law
items.summary: Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the decision was made following reviews of inspection reports, enforcement findings and representations submitted by the company, in accordance with existing laws and regulations. Onn Hafiz said the duration a premises had been operating did not mean all its activities were automatically compliant with licence requirements, adding that action must be taken once violations were identified. “When non-compliance is identified, action must be taken without compromise.

items.title: Rise in Social Visit Pass abuse triggers immigration crackdown
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, July 21 — The Immigration Department (JIM) has stepped up enforcement operations following a rise in the misuse of Social Visit Passes (SVPs) by foreign nationals, its director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said. He said Immigration records showed 1,279 arrests for SVP-related offences in 2023, rising to 2,644 in 2024 before easing slightly to 2,630 in 2025. We will continue pursuing those who abuse their immigration passes, particularly individuals who commit offences involving the misuse of these passes,” he told reporters after the 2026 Immigration Day celebration, themed ‘Charting a New Face of Immigration’, graced by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.

items.title: ‘I have nothing to do with it’: Azam Baki denies role in RM178m contract awarded to brother-linked firm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has denied having any involvement in the award of a RM178.29 million contract to a company linked to his brother, Datuk Nasir Baki. According to Sinar Harian , Azam said he was unaware of the matter and stressed that the contract award was a business matter involving the company and the relevant ministry. “It is up to the ministry to award the project to his company,” he said.

items.title: Motorists who ignore licence bans could face three-year prison term, says deputy transport minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Motorists who continue driving despite having their licences suspended or revoked by the court could face up to three years in prison under the Road Transport (Amendment) Bill 2026. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the amendment to Section 32 of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333) was aimed at increasing the existing penalties to encourage compliance with the law and reduce the risk of road accidents. “For electric motor vehicles, the amendment clarifies that such vehicles must have a clear, visible and untampered electric motor vehicle serial number and chassis number,” he said.

items.title: Tawau court jails tuition teacher 105 years, 70 strokes for repeatedly raping daughter; total sentence hits 280 years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A 53‑year‑old tuition teacher from Lahad Datu was sentenced by the Tawau Sessions Court to 105 years in prison and 70 strokes of the cane after pleading guilty to seven counts of repeatedly raping his eldest daughter between December 2025 and March 2026. Judge Ahmad Faizadh Yahaya handed down the punishment, ordering 15 years’ jail and 10 strokes for each charge, to run consecutively, Berita Harian reported. In total, he now faces 280 years in prison and 180 strokes of the cane.

items.title: Tamim Dahri pleads not guilty to charge of damaging sacred Hindu object
items.summary: LANGKAWI, July 21 — An activist pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of damaging a sacred Hindu object at an old temple site in March. Tamim Dahri Abdul Razak, 34, entered his plea before Magistrate Hemy Annerina Haja Mydin. However, lawyer Datuk Hanif Hassan, representing Tamim Dahri, applied for bail, stating that the offence under Section 295 of the Penal Code is bailable.

items.title: Teen suffers fractured skull, bleeding in head and ear after alleged assault in Cheras; four arrested
items.summary: Cheras district police chief ACP Mohd Rosdi Daud said police detected the circulation of a video recording of the incident on WhatsApp, Sinar Harian reported today. “A team from the Cheras district police headquarters’ Criminal Investigation Division subsequently arrested four local male teenagers, aged 14, to assist investigations,” he said in a statement today. Mohd Rosdi said police also seized a mobile phone belonging to one of the suspects, believed to have been used to record the incident.

items.title: Man detained in Tumpat after homemade air rifle found in car
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A 27-year-old man was detained after a homemade air rifle and 61 metal pellets were found in a car he was travelling in at Kampung Parit Bendang Morak, Tumpat, Kelantan, this morning. The man allegedly claimed the air rifle was used to hunt foxes, which he later sold in the Tumpat area. He said the team approached the vehicle, but the suspect sped off after noticing their presence.

items.title: Hadi defends PAS-Umno cooperation, cites need for Malay-Muslim unity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said the party was “forced” to cooperate with Umno as part of efforts to unite Malays and Muslims, warning that continued division could weaken their political position. In his latest “Minda Presiden PAS” article, Abdul Hadi rejected claims that cooperation between Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Barisan Nasional (BN) was merely political manoeuvring, saying such alliances should instead be viewed as efforts to strengthen unity. He said BA, formed in 1999, involved PAS, Parti Keadilan Nasional, Parti Rakyat Malaysia and DAP, but the coalition later weakened after disagreements emerged, including over PAS’ push for an Islamic state.

items.title: Housing Ministry prioritising urgent repairs for ageing PPR projects amid budget cuts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) is prioritising the maintenance of People’s Housing Programme (PPR) projects that are more than 10 years old, while focusing on critical defects that affect the safety and comfort of residents. Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said priority is given to issues involving lifts, roofs, water tanks and reticulation systems, sanitary piping, electrical wiring, as well as general repairs involving common property. Aiman Athirah said that under the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP), KPKT had spent RM159.1 million through Rolling Plans One to Five for the maintenance of high-rise strata PPR projects nationwide.

items.title: Man remanded two days from today over alleged insulting remarks against Negri Sembilan royal institution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A 52-year-old man has been remanded for two days to assist investigations into a social media post allegedly containing insulting remarks and comments deemed sensitive towards the Negri Sembilan royal institution. Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the man was remanded from today to facilitate investigations under Section 4 of the Sedition Act 1948, Section 505 of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, according to a report published by Kosmo! It was previously reported that the man was arrested at about 1pm yesterday over a post uploaded on the Threads application, which was believed to contain remarks that insulted and touched on sensitivities involving the Negri Sembilan royal institution.

items.title: High Court to decide on Papagomo sedition appeal on July 31
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The High Court today fixed July 31 to deliver its decision on the prosecution’s appeal against the acquittal of blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, on a sedition charge dating back to 2023. Judge Nurulhuda Nur’aini Mohamad Nor set the date after hearing submissions from Deputy Public Prosecutor (DPP) Mohamad Mustaffa P. Kunyalam and defence counsel Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, representing Wan Muhammad Azri. Earlier, Mohamad Mustaffa argued that the charge against Wan Muhammad Azri was not defective, despite the first prosecution witness, Hisham Sharudin, who was also the complainant, having withdrawn his police report.

items.title: Maxis probes unauthorised access to customer’s account after social media post
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Telecommunications company Maxis Bhd is investigating an incident involving a customer’s account that was circulated on social media, saying the matter was an isolated case involving unauthorised access. In a statement posted on its social media platforms today, Maxis said all access to customer accounts is fully logged and monitored. “Based on our findings, this is an isolated incident involving an unauthorised action.

items.title: Malaysia sees rise in foreigners misusing social visit passes to work, says Immigration Dept
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The Immigration Department has detected an increase in cases involving foreigners misusing social visit passes to work or conduct economic activities in Malaysia in recent years. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said 1,279 arrests were made in 2023, rising to 2,644 cases in 2024 and 2,630 cases last year, Kosmo! He said 451 arrests had been recorded as of July 15 this year.

items.title: Myanmar factory worker charged with murdering compatriot in Klang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A Myanmar factory worker was charged in the Magistrates’ Court in Klang today with the murder of his fellow countryman earlier this month. The accused, 32-year-old Maung Maung, nodded in understanding after the charge was read to him in Myanmar through an interpreter before Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat, according to a report published by Kosmo! According to the charge, Maung is accused of murdering Myo Than Naing at a premises along Jalan Tengah, Kampung Tengah Telok Gong, Port Klang, between 9.30pm and 10pm on July 5.

items.title: DBKL ramps up tree mitigation works to improve public safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is continuing tree mitigation works across several locations in the capital as part of ongoing efforts to improve public safety and reduce the risk posed by urban trees. In a Facebook post today, DBKL said this week’s works will focus on the parliamentary constituencies of Bukit Bintang, Lembah Pantai and Batu. The works will be carried out at Selera Medan Bunus (Lestari Niaga), Jalan Pantai Murni in Pantai Dalam, Jalan Pantai Permai in Bangsar, Jalan Haji Salleh in Sentul, Jalan Sinar Sentul in Sentul, and the JPK Towing Depot along Jalan Sentul.

items.title: Heavy rain, local factors caused Petaling Jaya flash floods, says Selangor exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The flash floods that hit several areas in Petaling Jaya on Saturday were likely caused by exceptionally heavy rainfall over a short period, which exceeded the capacity of existing drainage systems, according to Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim. Izham said preliminary assessments also pointed to localised factors as contributing to the flooding despite various flood mitigation measures, including the construction of the FAS Tunnel. Izham said the state government would continue strengthening its flood early warning system to ensure residents receive timely information and have adequate time to prepare.

items.title: No need for anyone to resign, PM decides which ministers come and go, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — The prime minister has the sole authority to decide whether a minister or member of the government administration should be removed from office, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. According to Berita Harian , Ahmad Zahid said there was no need for ministers or deputy ministers to resign if the prime minister had received verified information about a disciplinary breach. His remarks followed Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s warning that ministers, deputy ministers and federal agency heads would be asked to step down if they used their official positions to attack unity government partners during the Negri Sembilan state election campaign.

items.title: DPM Zahid denies claims of BN–Perikatan–GPS–GRS meeting, reaffirms support for unity government
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 21 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has dismissed viral claims that leaders from Barisan Nasional (BN), Perikatan Nasional (PN), Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) and Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) were scheduled to meet today. According to Berita Harian , the BN chairman said he had no knowledge of any such gathering and stressed that BN’s position remains firmly aligned with the Unity Government until the 16th General Election (GE16). Zahid, who is also Umno president, said BN’s stance is consistent with resolutions adopted at the Umno General Assembly.

items.title: Rights groups flag rising disinformation targeting Rohingya in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A wave of political disinformation targeting Malaysia’s Rohingya population has raised alarm among rights groups that say it is fuelling xenophobia and intimidation against the refugee community. More than 126,000 are Rohingya, making them the country’s largest refugee population. One Facebook post from a group called “Rohingya Malaysia” claimed there was a call for a Malaysian town to be handed over to a local refugee community.

items.title: Communications minister orders probe into alleged leak of influencer Khairul Aming’s personal data
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil has directed the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to obtain a full report on claims that influencer‑entrepreneur Khairul Aming’s personal data was accessed and leaked without authorisation by individuals linked to a telecommunications company. The issue surfaced after a Threads user publicly claimed to know specifics about Khairul Aming’s phone bill, including an alleged RM498 outstanding balance and details of additional digital purchases. Khairul Aming questioned how such information could be obtained and demanded clarification from the telco.

items.title: Accused of links to ‘love scam’ ring, nine Chinese nationals claim trial in Penang
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, July 21 — Nine Chinese nationals pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of participating in a criminal conspiracy linked to a “love scam” syndicate earlier this month. Deputy Public Prosecutor Ikmal Affendi Zulkifly proposed bail of RM10,000 for each accused with two local sureties. The court granted bail of RM8,000 each with two Malaysian sureties and ordered the accused to report to the Sungai Nibong Police Station every Thursday.

items.title: Sultan Nazrin warns weak institutions, poor governance can undermine nations
items.summary: IPOH, July 21 — A nation may possess abundant resources, a talented population and a rich culture, but still fail its people if its institutions are weak, its leaders unaccountable, or its politics consumed by self-interest rather than public service, said the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah. His Royal Highness said good governance in practical terms means the successful coordination of several essential elements in which institutions are strong enough to outlast any single leader or administration. “And it means a political culture in which disagreement is conducted with civility, the opposition is treated as a legitimate check rather than an enemy and power changes hands peacefully when the people so decide,” Sultan Nazrin said in his address at the 8th World Conference on Islamic Thought and Civilisation (WCIT): “Everyone Matters: Pride and Unity in Islam” here today.

items.title: King honours late Sultan Iskandar during annual ‘Hari Hol’ commemoration
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 21 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, visited Bukit Mahmoodiah Royal Mausoleum in Johor Bahru today in conjunction with the “Hari Hol” (death anniversary commemoration) of the late Sultan Iskandar. Accompanying him were the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, and the Raja Muda of Johor, Tunku Iskandar Tunku Ismail, according to a Facebook post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official page, which also shared photographs of the ceremony. His Majesty also visited the grave of his late son, Tunku Laksamana of Johor, Tunku Abdul Jalil Ibni Sultan Ibrahim.
